figures.caption: Figure 1: Validation Dice curves for 3D architectures with hyper-connection variants. Solid lines represent smoothed trends, while dashed lines indicate raw validation Dice across epochs.
Table 4: Parameter overhead introduced by Hyper-Connections (HC). All values are in millions (M). ∆ (%) denotes the relative increase over the corresponding baseline model.

Figure 13: Qualitative results for nnU-Net. Rows correspond to Ground Truth, Baseline, DHC×2, DHC×4, SHC×2, and SHC×4. Columns correspond to TC (red), WT (blue), and ET (green). Dice scores for each configuration are shown on the right. HC-based models produce segmentations that more closely match ground truth boundaries, particularly for ET.

Figure 3: Modality ablation under different connection mechanisms (nnUNet).

Table 2: Validation results with DHC (Dice %). Results shown are from a representative seed; multi-seed statistics are reported in Table 5.

Figure 16: Qualitative results for U-Netpp

Table 6: Dominant-modality Dice drop ∆dom and Dominant Sensitivity Enhancement (DSE) for all architecture–variant combinations. Dominant modality: T1ce for TC and ET; FLAIR for WT. DSE is computed relative to the Baseline (Eq. 15); a positive DSE indicates stronger dominantmodality reliance than the baseline. Best HC result per architecture per sub-region (highest ∆dom) is bold.
Table 3: Validation results with SHC (Dice %).
Figure 7: Modality ablation under different connection mechanisms (UNetpp).
Figure 5: Modality ablation under different connection mechanisms (VTUNet).

Table 5: BraTS 2021 validation results reported as mean ± standard deviation across multiple random seeds. Dice scores are reported for TC, WT, and ET, along with mean Dice. Bold indicates the best mean within each architecture group.
